what_it_does: A discovery report summarizing an autonomous overnight scan/extract/synthesis pass over a large corpus, quantifying file types, redundancy, built-vs-design status, semantic themes, contradictions, latent ideas, and a three-way intended/actual/required diagnosis across six rounds.
observations: Reports results of Rounds 0-6: type scan of 5,052 files, embedding clustering of 2,036 markdown files, semantic extraction of 1,760 files, code extraction of 382 files, and contradiction/latent clustering.
Quantifies that only ~487 files are code-ish and well under 10% is runnable; 2,227 are pure design-only and 1,155 are generated artifacts.
Identifies a runaway artifact: 288 near-identical copies of a REACTIVE_UPDATE_TIMEOUT gap note produced by a broken loop.
Notes heavy redundancy: vault-bootstrap templates copied across about seven sub-vaults and ElevenLabs files duplicated in two near-identical trees.
Finds content-workbench is the most implemented body, with 254 real-working code files, while the ElevenLabs Wizard proper is mostly design vault plus a small MCP server.
Surfaces pervasive claim-vs-reality mismatch as the dominant corpus pathology.
Includes operational infra notes about swapping GPU servers and restoring the production stack.
standouts: The corpus is overwhelmingly design/intent that was never built, with content-workbench appearing as the only near-shippable product.
A proto-Company principle-set is detected recurring across the design files.
A mathematical substrate theory is actually coded (Tarski fixed-point, sheaf/DAG proofs) but partially self-refuted and unresolved.
The legally-critical outbound-voice domains (telephony, compliance/consent, payment) are the thinnest domains.
conventions: Markdown report with hierarchical headings and round-based structure.
Uses quantified claims with counts and percentages.
Explicitly distinguishes discovery from interpretation ("Discovery, not conclusions", "my read — yours to confirm").
Cites artifact paths and infra commands inline.
Uses checkmark/status emojis and callout blocks.
concerns: The report makes broad corpus-wide claims that cannot be verified from this single file alone (expected for a findings document, but file-local verification is impossible).
Some phrasing ("the single highest-value action", "ship content-workbench first") is prescriptive, though flagged as the author's read awaiting confirmation.
Contains operational infra commands that could be hazardous if acted on without checking the current state.
notes: A quantified discovery autopsy of a scattered multi-product corpus, flagging that the only near-built product is content-workbench and that most material is design/process artifacts.
questions: Which exact files constitute the 254 real-working content-workbench code files?
What criteria classified a file as "built" vs "design-only" vs "unclear"?
How was the "proto-Company principle-set" extracted and what are the source file quotes?
What is the current state of the GPU servers mentioned in the infra note?
What is the relationship between this run-1 and the current Company pipeline?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a discovery findings report from an autonomous pipeline run, and the file does exactly that; the prescriptive "ship content-workbench first" call goes slightly beyond pure discovery but is explicitly framed as the author's read.
apparent_themes: Types/schema-driven routing and automated agent dispatch.
intention_signals: To make the scattered corpus legible by mechanically quantifying what is actually built, what is merely designed, where the real product leverage lies, and which latent intellectual assets might be harvested into the Company.
novelty: It is a meta-document: an autonomous, model-assisted audit of an earlier AI-generated project corpus that surfaces its own pathologies (runaway logs, claim-vs-reality mismatch) and reframes the named project as three tangled products rather than one.
contribution: Provides a structured, quantified baseline of an unreviewed corpus, including counts, redundancy signals, domain coverage gaps, reusable component candidates, latent/abandoned directions, and an explicit separation of discovery from interpretation — useful as a starting map for later fusion or migration work.
summary_for_embedding: Wizard run-1 discovery findings report quantifying a 5,052-file corpus as mostly unbuilt design artifacts, identifying content-workbench as the most implemented body, surfacing redundancy/pathologies and proto-Company principles, and offering a three-way intended/actual/required diagnosis.
intention_for_embedding: To document the results of an autonomous discovery pipeline pass over the ElevenLabs Wizard/Visual Designer corpus so later decisions about salvage, scope, and product direction can be grounded in counted evidence.
